title: Mouthwatering Crispy Japanese Noodle Ramen
description: A briny, crispy, and comforting Japanese dish featuring bouillon cubes and cure, made with ayurvedic-tamasic-friendly ingredients. This recipe is a twist on traditional ramen, with a crispy element added to the dish.
instructions: Bring a large pot of water to a boil and cook the ramen noodles according to the package instructions.
In a large skillet, heat the sesame oil over medium heat and saute the sliced ginger and minced garlic until fragrant.
Add the bouillon cubes to the skillet and stir to combine, then add 4 cups of water and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer the broth for 10 minutes, then stir in the soy sauce and miso paste.
Add the chopped bok choy to the broth and cook until wilted, about 2 minutes.
To assemble the dish, place some cooked noodles in a bowl, then add slices of the cooked ginger and green onions on top.
Ladle the hot broth over the noodles, then sprinkle crispy fried shallots over the top.
Garnish with additional green onions and crispy fried shallots if desired.
